title: pyrus × hazslinszkyana
intro: Pyrus × hazslinszkyana, also known as Hazslinszky's pear or the pyrus hazslinszkyana, is a hybrid plant species between Pyrus communis and Pyrus elaeagrifolia, typically found in Eastern Europe. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Pyrus × hazslinszkyana include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
